title: pulmonaria collina
intro: Pulmonaria collina, also known as mountain lungwort, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, Pulmonaria collina has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Pulmonaria collina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory problems and promote overall well-being.
